mappings.rationaleAI: Performing arts spanning Western, African, and Eastern traditions (West Asia, India, China, Japan) with prehistoric origins address arts of virtually every cultural region from antiquity through the modern era.

Literary history from Mesopotamian and Egyptian texts through medieval Islamic, Indian, East Asian, and European traditions addresses arts of the Western tradition (B), Asian peoples (C), and Middle Eastern and Islamic peoples (D).
Byzantine art, architecture, and literature spanning late antiquity through the medieval period address arts in antiquity as Early Christian art (B.1) and arts of the Middle Ages (B.2).
Ancient Egypt's monumental architecture, hieroglyphic writing, and polytheistic art directly address arts of ancient Egypt in antiquity (B.1).

Oceania's Aboriginal Australian artistic traditions and Pacific Islander cultures directly address arts of the Oceanian peoples (F), including Melanesia, Micronesia, Polynesia, and Australian Aboriginal peoples.
The Islamic Golden Age's literature, art, music, and architecture across Baghdad, Cordoba, and Cairo directly address arts of North Africa and of the Arab world (D.2).
The Middle Ages' theological, artistic, and architectural developments directly address arts of the Middle Ages in the Western tradition (B.2).
The Renaissance's visual arts, vernacular literature, and humanism directly address arts from the Renaissance to the present in Europe (B.3).
East Asia encompassing China, Japan, and Korea with Confucian, Buddhist, and Daoist cultural traditions directly addresses arts of East Asian peoples (C.1).
Suleiman's golden age of Ottoman architecture, literature, and arts addresses arts of the Arab world and Islamic peoples (D.2).
Charlemagne's Carolingian Renaissance and promotion of arts and learning address arts of the Middle Ages (B.2).
Akbar's merging of Timurid, Perso-Islamic, and Indian artistic traditions into a distinct Mughal style directly addresses arts of South Asian peoples including India (C.3).
The history of art surveying production across all cultures from prehistoric through contemporary directly addresses arts of every cultural region in the outline.
The history of architecture from Mesopotamian and Egyptian through Islamic, South Asian, East Asian, and European traditions addresses arts of Western (B), Asian (C), and Middle Eastern (D) peoples.
The Ottoman Empire's cultural production spanning Southeast Europe, West Asia, and North Africa addresses arts of the Islamic peoples and the Arab world (D.2).
The Tang dynasty's golden age of Chinese civilization in poetry, arts, and cultural exchange directly addresses arts of East Asian peoples, specifically China (C.1).
Italy's ancient Roman heritage and Renaissance artistic tradition address arts in antiquity (B.1) and arts from the Renaissance to the present (B.3).
Ancient Rome's literature, architecture, and visual arts directly address ancient Roman arts in antiquity (B.1).
The pre-Columbian era's Mesoamerican and South American civilizations directly address arts of Meso-American peoples (G.2) and South American peoples (G.3).
Nigeria's Nok culture, Benin Kingdom, and Oyo Empire artistic heritage directly address arts of West African cultures (E.5).
